body:
### What happened

`actions/download-artifact@v8` emits this warning on GitHub-hosted runners while downloading ordinary workflow artifacts:

```text
(node:...) [DEP0005] DeprecationWarning: Buffer() is deprecated due to security and usability issues. Please use the Buffer.alloc(), Buffer.allocUnsafe(), or Buffer.from() methods instead.
```

A current public reproduction is the `Download checksum fragments` step in https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/29465138404.

### Minimal workflow

```yaml
- uses: actions/download-artifact@v8
  with:
    pattern: checksums-*
    path: fragments
    merge-multiple: true
```

### Root cause evidence

The v8 bundle includes `@actions/artifact@6.2.1`, whose extraction dependency chain reaches `unzip-stream@0.3.4` -> `binary@0.3.0` / `buffers@0.1.1`; the bundled JavaScript still calls the deprecated `Buffer()` constructor. GitHub Actions now executes the action on Node 24, which surfaces DEP0005.

### Suggested fix

Upgrade or replace the extraction dependency chain and regenerate `dist/` so it contains no `Buffer()` constructor calls. A regression test can execute a small artifact download with `NODE_OPTIONS=--throw-deprecation` (or scan the generated bundle) to prevent recurrence.
title: v8 emits Node DEP0005 Buffer() deprecation warning on every download
